name: Pennsylvania Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: State Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Pennsylvania Department of State, Bureau of Professional and Occupational Affairs

address.description:
Pennsylvania State Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
P.O. Box 2649
Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

requiredDocuments.name: Official graduate transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: From an ASHA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

requiredDocuments.name: Clinical fellowship verification
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation of 1,260+ hours of supervised professional experience with 80% direct client contact.

requiredDocuments.name: Child abuse recognition training
requiredDocuments.description: 3 hours of Board-approved CE in child abuse recognition and reporting (required for initial licensure).

requiredDocuments.name: FBI fingerprint background check
requiredDocuments.description: Required for new applicants effective July 7, 2025. Pre-register through IdentoGO with service code provided during application.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Pennsylvania SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pennsylvania has one of the lowest SLP licensing fees in the country. The initial application fee is $50. Biennial renewal is $65, due by July 31 of odd-numbered years.

mainEntity.name: What is the new fingerprinting requirement for Pennsylvania SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Effective July 7, 2025, new health care practitioner applicants, including SLPs, must obtain a fingerprint-based FBI background check. Pre-register through IdentoGO using the service code provided during your application. This does not apply to renewals.

mainEntity.name: How many CE hours are required for Pennsylvania SLP renewal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pennsylvania requires 20 hours of continuing education every two years, including 2 hours of mandated child abuse reporter training. CE credits must be through ASHA or ASHA-approved entities.

mainEntity.name: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Pennsylvania?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
